provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Cybercrime and Computer Related Crimes Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Jurisdiction
Unauthorised access to a computer or computer system
provisions.content:
4. Unauthorised access to a computer or computer system
(1) Subject to subsections (2) and (3), any person who—
( a ) accesses the whole or any part of a computer or computer system, knowing that the access he or she intends to secure is unauthorised; or

definitions.definition: the Commissioner of Police appointed by the President in terms of section 112 of the Constitution
any device or material from which data is capable of being stored or reproduced, with or without the aid of any other device or material
data processing or the storage or retrieval of data

an electronic, magnetic or optical device or a group of interconnected or related devices, including the Internet, one or more of which, pursuant to a programme, performs automatic processing of data or any other function

the Director of the Directorate on Corruption and Economic Crime appointed by the President in terms of section 4 of the Corruption and Economic Crime Act (Cap. 08:05)<CS:"Hidden - Grey - 8_2"> or any person who may be appointed by the Minister by notice published in the Gazette
logic, control, arithmetic, deletion, storage and retrieval, and communication and telecommunication to, from or within a computer or computer system
any service involving the use of information and communication technology, including telecommunication services
any technology employed in the collecting, storing, using or sending out of information, including any technology involving the use of computers or any telecommunication system
the aural or other acquisition of the contents of any electronic, magnetic, optical or oral communication through the use of any device
any data by which a computer service, computer or computer system is capable of being obtained, accessed or used

an instruction or a set of instructions, expressed in words, codes, schemes or any other form, which is capable, when incorporated in a machine-readable medium, of causing a computer or computer system to achieve a particular task or result

property of any kind, nature or description, whether moveable or immoveable, tangible or intangible, and includes—
( a ) any currency whether or not the currency is legal tender in Botswana

any public or private person who—
( a ) provides to users of its services the ability to communicate by means of a computer or computer system
a person who lawfully uses the service of a service provider

any information, other than traffic or other data, contained in the form of computer data or any other form, that is held by a service provider and relating to any subscriber, by which can be established—
( a ) the type of communication service used, the technical provisions taken to use the communication service, and the period of such communication service

a transmission, emission or reception of signs, signals, writing, images, sounds or intelligence of any nature, by wire, radio, optical or other electromagnetic systems, whether or not such signs, signals, writing, images, sounds or intelligence have been subjected to rearrangement, computation or other processes, by any means, in the course of their transmission, emission or reception

any data—
( a ) that relates to communication by means of a computer or computer system
the type of service that is being used within a computer or computer system.
